bullets.text: Full-year CR 92.5, comfortably ahead of 96 target; pretax operating income $1.5B (+6% YOY); Commercial Lines CR 84 with mid-teen growth — rare combination; net income $1.3B ($2.15/share); ROE 17.2%.

NPW grew 10% to top $20 billion for first time; net premiums earned just under $20B; growth aided by 9 months of ASI property premium following April controlling-interest acquisition; Gainshare score 1.6 (highest in several years); $0.89/share variable dividend declared.

Agency auto returned to growth in H2 after years of disappointment: reversing erosion in policy counts while advancing premium growth; Direct auto achieved back-to-back years of low double-digit premium and just-under double-digit unit growth.

$400M debt offering completed in 2015; debt-to-total capital ~27%; $877M consumed by ARX transaction; $519M variable dividend paid; 7.3M shares repurchased (modest due to stock performance); investment portfolio $21B with ~1.6% total return ($455M interest/dividends).

Snapshot program continuing expansion with smartphone app planned for 2016 targeting broader policyholder reach; Snapshot used in real-time to analyze El Niño mileage/frequency patterns; vehicles viewed as 'moving IP addresses' — data integration central to competitive strategy.

Commercial Lines wrote north of $2B NPW — potentially capturing #1 commercial auto market share; Direct and Agency auto channels roughly equally balanced at ~50/50 split; Platinum (bundled agency) program introduced in select markets with encouraging early results.

Destination Era gaining institutional energy across all company areas; 'Acquire, Anchor, Bundle, Extend' model for customer lifecycle; addressable market could expand 50%+ for auto alone through Robinson graduation; products now span auto, property, umbrella, flood, classic car, pet, life, and more.

Platinum integration of ASI and Progressive in Agency channel launched — single bundled offering with coordinated policy periods and agent compensation; Direct channel multi-provider homeowners lineup via HomeQuote Explorer type approach; Progressive Advantage Agency expanding.

'Sprinkles are for Winners' organically went viral from Flo/Jamie commercial — adopted in song lyrics and sports mantras; media consumption shifting toward addressable digital channels from network TV; brand strategy evolving to reach specific audiences with greater yield measurement.

Long-term accident frequency declining 30+ years driven by vehicle safety technology; lower gas prices in 2015 increased mileage and partially offset frequency trend; autonomous vehicle features expected to continue reducing frequency long-term; Progressive using Snapshot data to distinguish weather vs. underlying trends.
